title: Wire run/sprint/eval orchestrators + per-skill reference copies + regenerate commands
description: Add the gated panel branch at the evaluator-spawn step of the three orchestrator SKILL.md sources (bober.run, bober.sprint, bober.eval), copy the canonical skills/shared/lens-panel.md into each skill's references/ dir so it inlines into the generated commands, regenerate the .claude/commands outputs byte-identically, and extend the drift gate to cover all three inlined copies. Off by default → exactly one full evaluator, byte-identical to today.

successCriteria.description: skills/bober.run/SKILL.md, skills/bober.sprint/SKILL.md, and skills/bober.eval/SKILL.md each gain a gated panel branch at their evaluator-spawn step: when config.evaluator.panel.enabled is true AND panel.lenses.length >= 2, follow the split fan-out (one deterministic-mode evaluator, then one lens-mode evaluator per lens, bounded by maxConcurrent), majority-vote the lens verdicts (fail-closed on tie), combine final.passed = deterministic.passed && reconciled.passed, and write a lensVerdicts array + evaluator='panel' into the saved eval-result JSON; OTHERWISE spawn exactly one full evaluator (MODE:full) as today. The branch references skills/shared/lens-panel.md (now inlined) for the protocol.

skills/shared/lens-panel.md is copied byte-identically to skills/bober.run/references/lens-panel.md, skills/bober.sprint/references/lens-panel.md, and skills/bober.eval/references/lens-panel.md (creating the bober.run/references/ dir).

.claude/commands/bober-run.md, bober-sprint.md, and bober-eval.md are regenerated using the EXACT inlineSkill concatenation format (SKILL.md content, then for each references/*.md in SORTED filename order: '

---

<!-- Reference: <file> -->

<content>'), so each includes the inlined lens-panel.md reference block and the panel branch. AUTHORITATIVE GATE (internal to this repo): the drift gate test recomputes each command from its SKILL.md + sorted references and asserts byte-equality with the committed .claude/commands/<name>.md. NOTE: `npm run update-all:check` targets EXTERNAL sync projects (sync-targets.json), not this repo, and already shows pre-existing drift from Sprint 2 — it is NOT a pass gate for this sprint; do not rely on it.

Off-by-default guarantee: with the panel disabled, the regenerated command prose drives exactly one full evaluator. Verified by git diff showing the panel branch is additive and gated, and the pre-existing single-evaluator spawn prose (Step 3f in bober-run, the eval spawn in bober-sprint, Step 3 in bober-eval) is otherwise intact.

The drift gate (src/orchestrator/lens-panel-parity.test.ts) is extended to assert (a) each of skills/bober.{run,sprint,eval}/references/lens-panel.md is byte-identical to skills/shared/lens-panel.md, AND (b) each .claude/commands/bober-{run,sprint,eval}.md equals its recomputed inline (SKILL.md + sorted references in the inlineSkill format). npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build, npx eslint src/ exit 0; the full vitest suite stays green with no new failures beyond the documented pre-existing skipped baseline.

nonGoals: Do not change the deterministic strategy set, the retry loop, the combine semantics beyond final = deterministic && reconciled, or the contract/spec schemas.
Do not edit the evaluator agent again (sprint 2 handled it) or src/orchestrator/eval-lenses.ts / reconciler.ts / eval-result.ts.
Do not wire the panel into any command beyond run/sprint/eval, nor into the planner/curator/generator.
Do not hand-edit the generated .claude/commands/*.md as the source of truth — regenerate them from the SKILL.md + references sources.
Do not modify scripts/update-all.mjs or src/cli/commands/init.ts inlining logic.
stopConditions: Stop when all three skills have the gated panel branch, the canonical reference is copied into all three references/ dirs, the three .claude/commands files are regenerated with no update-all:check drift, the drift gate covers all three copies, and typecheck/build/lint/tests are green.
Stop when changes are confined to the three SKILL.md files, the three references/lens-panel.md copies, the three regenerated .claude/commands/*.md files, and the drift-gate test.
definitionOfDone: All three native orchestrators (run/sprint/eval) gain a gated, additive panel branch implementing the split fan-out + majority-vote/fail-closed + lensVerdicts output, with the off path driving exactly one full evaluator byte-identically; the canonical lens-panel reference is copied into each skill's references/ and inlined into the regenerated commands; the drift gate proves all reference copies match the canonical AND each regenerated command equals its recomputed inline; typecheck/build/lint and the full suite are green.
assumptions: Each SKILL.md has a discrete evaluator-spawn step to wrap with the gated branch: bober.run Step 3f (Spawn the Evaluator Subagent), bober.sprint's evaluator spawn, and bober.eval Step 3 (Spawn the Evaluator Subagent).

scripts/update-all.mjs:inlineSkill defines the canonical inline format; the regenerated .claude/commands must match it byte-for-byte (the same format installClaudeCommands in src/cli/commands/init.ts uses). references/*.md are concatenated in sorted filename order.
Adding skills/bober.run/references/lens-panel.md is the first reference for bober.run; inlineSkill reads references/ if present (try/catch), so it will be picked up.

`npm run update-all:check` operates on EXTERNAL sync-target projects (scripts/sync-targets.json), NOT this repo, and already reports pre-existing drift after Sprint 2 changed the evaluator agent — it is NOT the pass gate for this sprint. The authoritative regeneration gate is a vitest assertion that recomputes each command (SKILL.md + sorted references in the inlineSkill format) and asserts byte-equality with the committed .claude/commands/<name>.md.
generatorNotes:
Read skills/shared/lens-panel.md (the protocol), skills/bober.run/SKILL.md, skills/bober.sprint/SKILL.md, skills/bober.eval/SKILL.md, and scripts/update-all.mjs (the inlineSkill function — note the exact concatenation format: `content += '

---

<!-- Reference: ' + refFile + ' -->

' + refContent` for each ref in sorted order). STEP 1 (wire the branch): in each of the three SKILL.md files, at the evaluator-spawn step, insert an ADDITIVE gated branch. Pattern: 'Read config.evaluator.panel. If panel.enabled AND panel.lenses.length >= 2 → run the PANEL flow (see the inlined Lens Panel reference below): spawn ONE bober-evaluator with MODE:deterministic (strategies once); then spawn one bober-evaluator per lens with MODE:lens:<name> (bounded by panel.maxConcurrent); collect each lens verdict; majority-vote (passed = passCount > failCount, FAIL-CLOSED on tie); set final.passed = deterministic.passed && reconciled.passed; save the eval-result with evaluator="panel" and a lensVerdicts:[{lens,passed,summary}] array. Else → spawn exactly ONE bober-evaluator (MODE:full) exactly as described below.' Leave the EXISTING single-evaluator spawn prose untouched as the else-branch body. Keep the insertion minimal and clearly gated. STEP 2 (copy reference): copy skills/shared/lens-panel.md verbatim to skills/bober.run/references/lens-panel.md, skills/bober.sprint/references/lens-panel.md, skills/bober.eval/references/lens-panel.md (create skills/bober.run/references/). STEP 3 (regenerate commands): regenerate .claude/commands/bober-run.md, bober-sprint.md, bober-eval.md from their SKILL.md + sorted references using the EXACT inlineSkill format (start with SKILL.md content; for each references/*.md in sorted filename order append `'

---

<!-- Reference: ' + refFile + ' -->

' + refContent`). Construct the concatenation to match inlineSkill byte-for-byte. DO NOT rely on `npm run update-all:check` as your gate — it syncs EXTERNAL projects (sync-targets.json), not this repo, and already shows pre-existing drift from Sprint 2; the authoritative gate is the vitest recomputation in STEP 4. STEP 4 (extend drift gate): in src/orchestrator/lens-panel-parity.test.ts, add (a) assertions that each skills/bober.{run,sprint,eval}/references/lens-panel.md equals skills/shared/lens-panel.md (read all via node:fs/promises + new URL, toBe); AND (b) for each of run/sprint/eval, recompute the inline (read skills/bober.<x>/SKILL.md, then readdir skills/bober.<x>/references, sort filenames, append each as the inlineSkill format) and assert it equals the committed .claude/commands/bober-<x>.md (toBe). This is the authoritative regeneration gate. Mirror scripts/update-all.mjs:inlineSkill exactly for the recomputation. Conventional commit: bober(sprint-3): wire run/sprint/eval panel branch + per-skill reference copies + regenerate commands. GIT HYGIENE: stage only your files with explicit paths; never `git add -A`. Branch is the feature branch — never main. NOTE: this sprint changes generated .claude/commands/*.md AND .gitignore-tracked artifacts may already be dirty from earlier unrelated work — only stage the six source files + three regenerated commands + the test.
evaluatorNotes: For each of the three SKILL.md files: confirm an additive, clearly gated panel branch exists at the evaluator-spawn step (enabled && lenses>=2 → split fan-out + majority-vote/fail-closed + lensVerdicts; else → one full evaluator). CRITICAL off-path check: `git diff` each SKILL.md and confirm the pre-existing single-evaluator spawn prose is intact (the new branch is purely additive and the else-path is the old prose). Confirm skills/bober.{run,sprint,eval}/references/lens-panel.md each equal skills/shared/lens-panel.md (diff directly). Confirm the three .claude/commands/*.md were regenerated and now contain the inlined '<!-- Reference: lens-panel.md -->' block and the panel branch. DO NOT use `npm run update-all:check` as a pass/fail gate — it targets EXTERNAL sync projects (sync-targets.json), not this repo, and already shows pre-existing drift from Sprint 2; running it is informational only. The AUTHORITATIVE regeneration gate is the extended drift test (src/orchestrator/lens-panel-parity.test.ts): confirm it (a) asserts each skills/bober.{run,sprint,eval}/references/lens-panel.md equals skills/shared/lens-panel.md, AND (b) recomputes each command from SKILL.md + sorted references (inlineSkill format) and asserts byte-equality with the committed .claude/commands/bober-{run,sprint,eval}.md. Run it and confirm all assertions green; reason that it would fail if a command were not regenerated or a reference copy diverged. Run npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build, npx eslint src/ (exit 0) and the full npx vitest run (tolerate only the pre-existing skipped baseline). Confirm no changes leaked into eval-lenses.ts, reconciler.ts, eval-result.ts, the evaluator agent, or update-all.mjs.
